title: lycopersicon hirsutum var. agrimoniifolium
intro: Lycopersicon hirsutum var. agrimoniifolium, also known as hairy tomato vine or hairy nightshade, is a plant species within the nightshade family that is native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, colds, and digestive issues, due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used to make teas and infusions, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Lycopersicon hirsutum var. agrimoniifolium is often combined with other herbs to create tonics and remedies for respiratory issues and digestive problems.
